summary: This brief post warns readers to be skeptical of Ello, a social media platform marketed as a privacy-friendly alternative. O'Neil directs readers to journalist Meredith Broussard's analysis of Ello's security policy, which reveals concerning fine print. The post serves as a quick cautionary note about trusting new platforms' privacy claims at face value.
key_points: Ello's reputation as a 'safe' social media platform may be undeserved
The fine print in Ello's security policy contains troubling provisions
Users should read and understand platform security policies before trusting them with their data
money_quotes: If you think Ello is the newest safest social media platform, you might want to think again.
key_insight: Privacy-focused marketing claims from new social media platforms deserve skeptical scrutiny of their actual policies.
